source.title: KODAK PROFESSIONAL TRI-X 400 Film — Technical Data

profiles.name: Official table
notes: Source data sheet states no exposure adjustment is required from 1/1,000 sec to 1/10 sec.

Source data sheet also lists +1/3 stop at 1/10,000 sec for short-exposure (high-speed) photography; the launch reciprocity catalog scopes long-exposure data only and preserves this fact as a note rather than a quantified anchor.
No exposure adjustment required from 1/1000 sec to 1/10 sec per the published data sheet.

Source publishes +1/3 stop at 1 sec without a corrected time; the corrected time is derived (1 sec × 2^(1/3) ≈ 1.26 sec) so this anchor joins the same log-log estimation family as the 10 sec / 100 sec rows.

source.title: KODAK PROFESSIONAL EKTACHROME E100 Film — Technical Data

notes: No correction required for exposures of 120 sec or less.
120 to 1000 sec → +1/2 stop.

profiles.name: Official formula anchored to 1 s threshold

notes: No correction required from 1/4000 sec to 1 sec.
Tc = Tm^P, P ≈ 1.1821. Threshold-anchored constrained log-log fit through Fujifilm's published 4/8/16/32 sec source references. 64 sec is not recommended.
4 sec → +1/3 stop, 5M.
8 sec → +1/2 stop, 7.5M.
16 sec → +2/3 stop, 10M.
32 sec → +1 stop, 12.5M.

profiles.name: Official formula anchored to 60 s threshold

notes: No correction required from 1/4000 sec to 1 min.

Tc = 60 × (Tm / 60)^P, P ≈ 1.2667. Threshold-anchored constrained log-log fit through the 60 s no-correction boundary and Fujifilm's published 2-min (+1/3 stop) and 4-min (+1/2 stop) references. Inputs above 240 sec extrapolate the same curve outside the published source range.
2 min → +1/3 stop, 2.5M.
4 min → +1/2 stop, 2.5M.

profiles.name: Official formula anchored to 240 s reference

notes: No correction required from 1/4000 sec to 128 sec.
Tc = 128 × (Tm / 128)^P, P ≈ 1.3676. Anchored to Fujifilm's published 4-min (240 s) +1/3-stop reference. 8 min is not recommended.
4 min → +1/3 stop, 2.5G.
